hook.text: A ruby and a sapphire are the same mineral: corundum. Trace amounts of different atoms create different colors.

buildup.text: Gemstones are minerals valued for beauty, durability, and rarity. They must be hard enough to resist scratching.

discovery.text: Chromium makes corundum red (ruby). Titanium and iron make it blue (sapphire). Chemistry paints the gems.

twist.text: Diamonds aren't the rarest gemstones. Alexandrite and painite are far rarer. Marketing made diamonds seem supreme.

climax.text: Gemstones are minerals that survived extreme conditions beautifully. Their value comes from geology, not just looks.

punchline.text: Gems are minerals that won the beauty lottery.

quiz.question: What mineral produces both rubies and sapphires?
